title: "bid (AuctionBidder)"

stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB
    [*] --> q_guard: whenNotPaused, onlyRole(AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE)
    q_guard --> q_load: auction = s_auction
    q_load --> q_get_asset: assetOut = auction.getAssetOut()
    q_get_asset --> q_check_solution: solution.length > 0
    q_check_solution --> q_encode: true
    q_check_solution --> q_approve: false
    q_encode --> q_bid: data = abi.encode(solution)
    q_approve --> q_bid: forceApprove(auction, getAssetOutAmount(assetIn, amount, block.timestamp))
    q_bid --> q_balance: auction.bid(assetIn, amount, data)
    q_balance --> q_check_bal: assetOutBalance = IERC20(assetOut).balanceOf(this)
    q_check_bal --> q_check_receiver: assetOutBalance > 0
    q_check_bal --> q_final: assetOutBalance == 0
    q_check_receiver --> q_transfer: receiver != address(0)
    q_check_receiver --> q_final: receiver == address(0)
    q_transfer --> q_final: safeTransfer(receiver, assetOutBalance)
    q_final --> [*]

    note right of q_approve
        INV-001: When no solution provided, contract pre-approves exact assetOut amount for auction
        INV-002: forceApprove amount is based on getAssetOutAmount at block.timestamp
    end note

    note right of q_bid
        INV-003: auction.bid may trigger auctionCallback if data is non-empty (re-entrant call)
        INV-004: Callback executes arbitrary calls via _multiCall before returning
    end note

    note right of q_transfer
        INV-005: Leftover assetOut tokens forwarded to receiver if set and balance > 0
        INV-006: If receiver is address(0), leftover tokens remain in contract
    end note

---
title: "_call (Caller.sol)"
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB
    [*] --> q_lowcall: (success, response) = target.call(data)
    q_lowcall --> q_check_success: evaluate success
    q_check_success --> q_return: success == true
    q_check_success --> q_check_response: success == false
    q_check_response --> q_bubble_revert: response.length > 0
    q_check_response --> q_revert_generic: response.length == 0
    q_bubble_revert --> [*]: revert(response)
    q_revert_generic --> [*]: revert LowLevelCallFailed()
    q_return --> [*]: return response

    note right of q_lowcall
        INV-001: target.call(data) performs arbitrary external call with no gas limit restriction
        INV-002: return value success must be checked before using response
    end note

    note right of q_check_response
        INV-003: if call reverts with data, original revert reason is bubbled up via assembly
        INV-004: if call reverts without data, generic LowLevelCallFailed error is raised
    end note

1→---
     2→title: "_multiCall (Caller.sol)"
     3→---
     4→stateDiagram-v2
     5→    direction TB
     6→    [*] --> q_check_empty: check calls.length
     7→    q_check_empty --> q_revert_empty: calls.length == 0
     8→    q_check_empty --> q_alloc: calls.length > 0
     9→    q_revert_empty --> [*]: revert Errors.EmptyList()
    10→    q_alloc --> q_iter: returnData = new bytes[](calls.length), i = 0
    11→    q_iter --> q_call: i < calls.length
    12→    q_iter --> q_return: i >= calls.length
    13→    q_call --> q_increment: returnData[i] = _call(calls[i].target, calls[i].data)
    14→    q_increment --> q_iter: i = i + 1

title: "_multiCall (Caller.sol)"
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB
    [*] --> q_check_empty: check calls.length
    q_check_empty --> q_revert_empty: calls.length == 0
    q_check_empty --> q_alloc: calls.length > 0
    q_revert_empty --> [*]: revert Errors.EmptyList()
    q_alloc --> q_iter: returnData = new bytes[](calls.length), i = 0
    q_iter --> q_call: i < calls.length
    q_iter --> q_return: i >= calls.length
    q_call --> q_increment: returnData[i] = _call(calls[i].target, calls[i].data)
    q_increment --> q_iter: i = i + 1
    q_return --> [*]: return returnData

    note right of q_check_empty
        INV-001: empty calls array is rejected with EmptyList revert
    end note

    note right of q_iter
        INV-002: loop iterates exactly calls.length times
        INV-003: each iteration delegates to _call which may revert, aborting entire multiCall
        INV-004: no duplicate or reentry guard - if calls[i].target re-enters, state may be inconsistent
    end note

    note right of q_call
        INV-005: returnData array indices map 1-to-1 with calls array indices
    end note

**Batch: 1 item | AuctionBidder.sol + Caller.sol | 9 subgraphs**

## Phase A: Trust Model Analysis

**Actors identified:**
1. **Bidders** (UNTRUSTED) — AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE holders calling `bid()`
2. **Auction Contract** (SEMI_TRUSTED) — `s_auction` that calls back via `auctionCallback`
3. **Admin** (TRUSTED) — DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE for `withdraw()`, `_setAuction`, `_setReceiver`
4. **External call targets** (UNTRUSTED) — arbitrary targets invoked via `_multiCall` during callback
5. **Receiver** — passive recipient of forwarded assetOut tokens

**Trust Boundaries:**
- TB-1: Bidder → `bid()` (role-gated, but solution data is attacker-controlled)
- TB-2: Auction → `auctionCallback()` (auth: msg.sender == s_auction && from == this)
- TB-3: AuctionBidder → external targets via `_multiCall` (arbitrary calls, no reentrancy guard)
- TB-4: Admin → `withdraw()` (privileged fund extraction)

**STRIDE Analysis key findings:**
- **Spoofing**: auctionCallback has dual auth check (msg.sender + from). Robust.
- **Tampering**: _multiCall executes arbitrary calls from decoded callback data. Solution parameter flows: bid(solution) → auction.bid(data) → callback(data) → _multiCall(decode(data)). Role holder controls external call targets.
- **Elevation of Privilege**: During _multiCall, contract identity is used for arbitrary calls — can set token approvals to arbitrary spenders that persist after callback.
- **DoS**: A single failing _multiCall target reverts entire bid. No gas limit on individual calls.
- **Reentrancy**: _multiCall has no reentrancy guard (INV-004). External call targets could re-enter.
